name: Distributed lock with fencing tokens
task: Create /tmp/eval_dl/dl.py with class DistributedLock. Method acquire(owner_id, ttl, now) returns a monotonically increasing fencing token (the first token ever issued is 1, each subsequent grant is strictly larger) if the lock is free or expired; otherwise None. The lock is expired at time `now` iff now >= (grant_time + ttl). release(owner_id, token) returns True iff the (owner, token) pair is the current holder (and clears the lock); otherwise False. renew(owner_id, token, ttl, now) returns True and resets the expiry to now+ttl iff the caller still owns it (matching owner and token) and it has not yet expired; otherwise returns False.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_dl/dl.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); L = mod.DistributedLock(); t1 = L.acquire('node1', ttl=10, now=0); assert t1 == 1, f'first token {t1} must be 1'; assert L.acquire('node2', ttl=10, now=1) is None; assert not L.release('node2', t1); assert not L.renew('node2', t1, ttl=10, now=2), 'non-owner renew must fail'; assert L.renew('node1', t1, ttl=10, now=5), 'owner renew within ttl must succeed'; assert L.acquire('node2', ttl=10, now=14) is None, 'renew should have extended expiry to 15'; assert L.release('node1', t1); assert not L.renew('node1', t1, ttl=10, now=16), 'renew after release must fail'; t2 = L.acquire('node2', ttl=10, now=16); assert t2 > t1; assert not L.renew('node2', t2, ttl=10, now=40), 'renew after expiry must fail'; t3 = L.acquire('node3', ttl=10, now=100); assert t3 > t2; print('OK')
